metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students develop advanced skills in CNC programming, CAD/CAM design-to-production workflows, and digital fabrication technologies. This course emphasizes computer numerical control programming, 3D design, laser cutting, and 3D printing applications.
metadata.general_description: CNC programming, CAD/CAM workflows, laser cutting, and 3D printing technologies.

big_ideas.statement: Digital fabrication transforms designs into physical products through computer-controlled machines
big_ideas.elaboration: CAM software bridges the gap between digital designs and physical production.

big_ideas.statement: CNC programming requires understanding machine capabilities, tool paths, and manufacturing constraints
big_ideas.elaboration: Effective CNC programs optimize efficiency, quality, and tool life.

big_ideas.statement: CAD/CAM integration enables efficient design-to-production workflows
big_ideas.elaboration: Seamless data flow from design through fabrication reduces errors and increases efficiency.

big_ideas.statement: Emerging technologies like 3D printing and laser cutting expand possibilities for product creation
big_ideas.elaboration: New fabrication methods enable rapid prototyping, customization, and innovative designs.

i_can_statements: I can design considering machine capabilities
I can optimize designs for production
I can plan manufacturing sequences
I can design for multiple fabrication methods

competencies.raw: Design CNC programs and tool paths
i_can_statements: I can plan efficient tool paths
I can select appropriate tools
I can optimize speeds and feeds
I can design for quality and efficiency

competencies.raw: Design for 3D printing and laser cutting
i_can_statements: I can design 3D printable models
I can optimize for laser cutting
I can design assembly and finishing
I can plan material selection
I can design CAD/CAM workflows
I can plan multi-step production
I can design for batch production
I can optimize for speed and quality

competencies.raw: Apply CAD modeling and design skills
i_can_statements: I can create 3D models for fabrication
I can prepare models for export
I can modify and iterate designs
I can validate designs for production

competencies.raw: Apply CNC programming and machine operation
i_can_statements: I can write and optimize CNC code
I can set up and run CNC machines
I can troubleshoot machining problems
I can measure and verify results
I can prepare models for 3D printing
I can operate 3D printers
I can post-process printed parts
I can troubleshoot printing issues

competencies.raw: Apply laser cutting and engraving techniques
i_can_statements: I can design for laser cutting
I can operate laser equipment
I can optimize cuts for quality
I can combine cutting with engraving

competencies.raw: Use CAD and CAM software effectively
i_can_statements: I can use professional CAD software
I can use CAM software for tool paths
I can generate CNC code from CAD
I can integrate CAD/CAM workflows
I can operate mills and lathes
I can use routers and cutters
I can set up work and tools
I can maintain equipment

competencies.raw: Use 3D printing and rapid prototyping technologies
i_can_statements: I can use various 3D printing technologies
I can manage print settings
I can post-process prints
I can use additive manufacturing

competencies.raw: Use laser cutting and digital fabrication technologies
i_can_statements: I can operate laser cutters
I can manage laser software
I can combine multiple fabrication methods
I can automate and optimize production
